name: Indexer Client Package Examples
description: Create runnable examples demonstrating the @algorandfoundation/algokit-indexer-client package. IMPORTANT: After implementing each example, you MUST run it to verify it works: `npx tsx <example>.ts`. The indexer-client package provides a typed, promise-based client for the Algorand Indexer REST API, enabling queries for accounts, transactions, assets, applications, and blocks.

userStories.title: Infrastructure Setup - Shared Utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared utilities and constants for all indexer_client examples so that examples have consistent setup and helper functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/shared/constants.ts with INDEXER_CONFIG (port 8980), ALGOD_CONFIG (port 4001), and KMD_CONFIG (port 4002) for LocalNet connection

Create examples/indexer_client/shared/utils.ts with: printHeader(), printStep(), printInfo(), printSuccess(), printError(), formatAlgo(), shortenAddress(), createIndexerClient(), createAlgodClient(), createAlgorandClient(), getFundedAccount(), createRandomAccount()
Create examples/indexer_client/package.json referencing file:../../dist for @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils
Create examples/indexer_client/tsconfig.json with ESM module settings
Create examples/indexer_client/README.md documenting all indexer_client examples
npm install runs without errors in examples/indexer_client directory
TypeScript compiles without errors: npx tsc --noEmit

userStories.title: Health Check Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to check indexer health status using healthCheck().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/01-health-check.ts
Demonstrate healthCheck() to check if the indexer service is healthy
Display the health check response including version, dbAvailable, isMigrating, message, round, and errors
Handle and display any connection errors gracefully
Run the example with `npx tsx 01-health-check.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Account Lookup Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to lookup account information using lookupAccountById() and searchForAccounts().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/02-account-lookup.ts
Demonstrate lookupAccountById(address) to get detailed account information
Display account fields: address, amount, amountWithoutPendingRewards, minBalance, status, round
Demonstrate searchForAccounts() to search for accounts with filters
Show filtering by currencyGreaterThan, onlineOnly, and limit parameters
Handle the case where account is not found
Run the example with `npx tsx 02-account-lookup.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Account Assets Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to query account asset holdings using lookupAccountAssets() and lookupAccountCreatedAssets().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/03-account-assets.ts
Create a test asset using AlgorandClient for setup
Demonstrate lookupAccountAssets(address) to get all assets held by an account
Display asset holding fields: assetId, amount, isFrozen, optedInAtRound
Demonstrate lookupAccountCreatedAssets(address) to get assets created by an account
Display created asset params: creator, total, decimals, name, unitName
Show pagination using limit and next parameters
Run the example with `npx tsx 03-account-assets.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Account Applications Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to query account application relationships using lookupAccountCreatedApplications() and lookupAccountAppLocalStates().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/04-account-applications.ts
Deploy a test application using AlgorandClient for setup
Demonstrate lookupAccountCreatedApplications(address) to get applications created by an account
Display application params: id, creator, approvalProgram, clearStateProgram, globalStateSchema, localStateSchema
Demonstrate lookupAccountAppLocalStates(address) to get application local states
Display local state key-value pairs for opted-in applications
Show pagination using limit and next parameters
Run the example with `npx tsx 04-account-applications.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Account Transactions Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to query an account's transaction history using lookupAccountTransactions().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/05-account-transactions.ts
Create some transactions using AlgorandClient for setup (payments, asset transfers)
Demonstrate lookupAccountTransactions(address) to get transaction history
Display transaction fields: id, txType, sender, fee, confirmedRound, roundTime
Show filtering by txType (pay, axfer, appl), minRound, maxRound
Show filtering by beforeTime and afterTime
Demonstrate currencyGreaterThan and currencyLessThan filters for amount
Note: Results are returned newest to oldest for account transactions
Run the example with `npx tsx 05-account-transactions.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Transaction Lookup Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to lookup a single transaction by ID using lookupTransactionById().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/06-transaction-lookup.ts
Create a transaction using AlgorandClient and capture its txId
Demonstrate lookupTransactionById(txId) to get full transaction details
Display comprehensive transaction fields: id, txType, sender, fee, firstValid, lastValid
Display type-specific fields based on transaction type (payment: receiver, amount; asset transfer: assetId, assetAmount)
Display confirmation info: confirmedRound, roundTime, intraRoundOffset
Handle the case where transaction is not found
Run the example with `npx tsx 06-transaction-lookup.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Transaction Search Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to search for transactions with various filters using searchForTransactions().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/07-transaction-search.ts
Create several different transaction types for setup
Demonstrate searchForTransactions() with default parameters
Show filtering by txType to find specific transaction types
Show filtering by sigType (sig, msig, lsig)
Show filtering by address with addressRole (sender, receiver)
Show filtering by round range (minRound, maxRound)
Show filtering by time range (beforeTime, afterTime)
Show filtering by applicationId to find app calls
Note: Results are oldest to newest, except when using address filter
Run the example with `npx tsx 07-transaction-search.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Asset Lookup Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to lookup and search for assets using lookupAssetById() and searchForAssets().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/08-asset-lookup.ts
Create test assets using AlgorandClient for setup
Demonstrate lookupAssetById(assetId) to get asset information
Display asset params: index, creator, total, decimals, name, unitName, url, metadataHash
Display manager addresses: manager, reserve, freeze, clawback
Demonstrate searchForAssets() to search for assets
Show filtering by name, unit, creator
Show filtering by assetId for exact match
Handle the case where asset is not found or destroyed
Run the example with `npx tsx 08-asset-lookup.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Asset Balances Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to lookup all holders of an asset using lookupAssetBalances().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/09-asset-balances.ts
Create a test asset and distribute it to multiple accounts
Demonstrate lookupAssetBalances(assetId) to get all holders
Display balance fields: address, amount, isFrozen, optedInAtRound
Show filtering by currencyGreaterThan and currencyLessThan
Show includeAll parameter to include accounts with 0 balance (opted-in but no holding)
Demonstrate pagination for assets with many holders
Run the example with `npx tsx 09-asset-balances.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Asset Transactions Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to lookup transactions for a specific asset using lookupAssetTransactions().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/10-asset-transactions.ts
Create a test asset and perform several asset transactions (opt-in, transfer, freeze)
Demonstrate lookupAssetTransactions(assetId) to get all transactions for an asset
Display transaction details relevant to asset operations
Show filtering by address and addressRole (sender, receiver, freeze-target)
Show filtering by txType for specific asset operations (acfg, axfer, afrz)
Show filtering by round and time ranges
Note: Results are returned oldest to newest
Run the example with `npx tsx 10-asset-transactions.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Application Lookup Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to lookup and search for applications using lookupApplicationById() and searchForApplications().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/11-application-lookup.ts
Deploy test applications using AlgorandClient for setup
Demonstrate lookupApplicationById(appId) to get application details
Display application params: id, creator, approvalProgram, clearStateProgram
Display state schema: globalStateSchema, localStateSchema
Display global state key-value pairs if present
Demonstrate searchForApplications() to search for applications
Show filtering by creator address
Show includeAll parameter to include deleted applications
Handle the case where application is not found
Run the example with `npx tsx 11-application-lookup.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Application Logs Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to lookup application logs using lookupApplicationLogsById().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/12-application-logs.ts
Deploy an application that emits logs and call it to generate log entries
Demonstrate lookupApplicationLogsById(appId) to get application logs
Display log entry fields: txId, round, logs (base64 decoded)
Show filtering by txId to get logs from a specific transaction
Show filtering by minRound and maxRound
Show filtering by senderAddress to get logs from calls by a specific account
Demonstrate pagination for applications with many log entries
Run the example with `npx tsx 12-application-logs.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Application Boxes Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to query application boxes using searchForApplicationBoxes() and lookupApplicationBoxByIdAndName().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/13-application-boxes.ts
Deploy an application that uses box storage and create several boxes
Demonstrate searchForApplicationBoxes(appId) to list all box names
Display box names from the response
Demonstrate lookupApplicationBoxByIdAndName(appId, boxName) to get specific box value
Show how to properly encode box names using Uint8Array
Display box value after decoding from base64
Handle the case where application has no boxes
Demonstrate pagination for applications with many boxes
Run the example with `npx tsx 13-application-boxes.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Block Lookup Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to lookup block information using lookupBlock().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/14-block-lookup.ts
Demonstrate lookupBlock(roundNumber) to get full block details
Display block fields: round, timestamp, genesisId, genesisHash, previousBlockHash
Display block header info: seed, txnCommitments, participationUpdates
Show transactions included in the block if any
Demonstrate headerOnly parameter to get only block header without transactions
Get a recent block round from the indexer and fetch it
Handle the case where block is not found
Run the example with `npx tsx 14-block-lookup.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Block Headers Search Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to search for block headers using searchForBlockHeaders().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/15-block-headers.ts
Demonstrate searchForBlockHeaders() to search block headers
Display block header fields: round, timestamp, proposer (if available)
Show filtering by minRound and maxRound
Show filtering by beforeTime and afterTime
Show filtering by proposers array to find blocks proposed by specific accounts
Note: Results are returned in ascending round order
Demonstrate pagination for fetching multiple block headers
Run the example with `npx tsx 15-block-headers.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Pagination Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to properly handle pagination across multiple indexer endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/16-pagination.ts
Demonstrate pagination pattern using limit and next parameters
Show how to iterate through all pages of results for searchForTransactions()
Show pagination with searchForAccounts()
Show pagination with searchForAssets()
Implement a generic pagination helper function
Display total count of items across all pages
Demonstrate early termination when a condition is met
Handle the case where there are no results
Run the example with `npx tsx 16-pagination.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry

userStories.title: Verification Script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script to run all indexer_client examples and verify they work.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/indexer_client/verify-all.sh bash script
Script runs each example in order (01 through 16)
Script reports success/failure for each example
Script exits with non-zero code if any example fails
Add npm script 'verify-all' to package.json
All 16 examples pass when running ./verify-all.sh
